commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Multitude. Hebrew and Septuagint, "army. "(Calmet)
Silly. Literally, "feeble. "(Haydock)
Sacrifice, at the dedication. (Tirinus)
Day, in so short a time, as their present vigorous proceedings seem to promise, notwithstanding their feeble condition, and the paucity of their numbers. (Haydock)
But no: we shall have time enough to hinder them, (Menochius) if the nature of their materials do it not for us. (Haydock)
Raise. Hebrew, "revive "a word used for reparations of walls (Calmet) (Delrio, adag. 221.)
Septuagint, "heal. "God's providence did not permit the enemy to rage, till the work was greatly advanced; so infidels laugh at the attempts of poor priests to restore religion, which nevertheless flourishes. (Worthington)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
